reviews.review_title: App stopped working no email support
reviews.review_description: The Reakt app itself is very fun and I was using it on an everyday basis but after the most recent update it stopped working which was showing a black screen. I sent an email to customer service and got no response back after a few days. I want a refund back if I cant even use the app and I cant do it because I have played over 2 hours. Don't buy this application because you wont get support to fix it.

developer_response.body:
Hi there — we’re really sorry for the frustration.

A small number of users experienced a black screen after the latest update, but this has been resolved. Most users were able to fix the issue on their own by uninstalling and reinstalling the app. 

We also apologize for the delayed email response during a recent support system update. That issue has been fixed. Please reach out to us at support@reakttrainer.com
 so we can help make this right. 

We appreciate you trying Reakt and are glad you found it to be enjoyable.

reviews.review_title: Most of the time it’s server is down
reviews.review_description: It’s show frustrating, when you have time to launch the app and you see server down error.

developer_response.body: The way our system runs is something we take seriously, and we're sorry Reakt didn't deliver that for you. Thank you for letting us know there was an issue. We've fixed the problem and it should be running as designed for you.

reviews.review_description:
Very intensive and interesting trainer!
Don’t think that is so heavy intensive, until you play 5 minutes and pulse at nearly 160.
Progess can be tracked by grow up reaction time in milliseconds.

App have no updates for 4 years?
New versions or features?

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the great review!

We love hearing that you’re feeling the intensity — NeuroTrainer is designed to challenge both your brain and your body, and that heart rate spike proves it’s working. 💪🧠

Tracking your reaction time improvements in milliseconds is one of our favorite ways to see real progress, too. Keep pushing — and thanks for being part of the NeuroTrainer community!

reviews.review_description:
I was very excited by this App, however despite uninstalling/reinstalling multiple times, when I start the app I get the logo followed by a black screen that doesn't go away. I'm using a Quest 3 that is up to date.

I'm refunding for now, but if this starts working in the future I'd be willing to revisit it.
Love it. I hope the developers will continue to develop it with  additional exercises to help improve response and increase coordination. Really good.

I love this software, I have been using it for a year now and I have to admit that I am seeing crazy results in my reflexes. Calling it a game is misleading, it is not for everyone, just read the negative reviews where it is criticized for the same reasons that make it unique for me. The dynamics of the game are simple, there are balls to intercept and bombs to avoid. It is possible to track the bombs by clicking the trigger, in this way they slow down their speed. There are many modes and the recommended workouts push on your deficiencies in order to improve you. The weekly challenges are very competitive but you have to be ready to sweat! For me it is 5 stars!

This app is OK… but a little stupid. There are virtually NO instructions and the instructions in the beginning are hard to make out. There is no way to repeat them and you can sit there and do the WRONG thing for an HOUR because you didn’t hear the instructions correctly and NOTHING happens or changes or does ANYTHING. There is no way to speed it up or slow it down or Do Anything. This app has potential but it is as yet unrealized

developer_response.body:
You can repeat the tutorials by going to: 
Custom Training > Visual Concentration > Tutorial 
Custom Training > Athletic Awareness > Tutorial

reviews.review_description:
This app is NOT a game, it is a reflex/concentration trainer.

Some paddles are red and eject missiles the other paddles are yellow and eject yellow balls. You have to catch the yellow balls and you have to move your head to avoid the red missiles. To make it a reflex/concentration exercise they flip the paddles over and the paddles start moving so that you have to remember and track the red paddles.

You need to remember which paddles eject missiles and pull the trigger as soon as it starts blinking (you have 1sec (=1000 msec) to pull the trigger before the paddle flips over and fires a missile.  If you are able to pull the trigger in time the missile will be fired slowly and you'll have more time to move your head out of the way.  

If you pull the trigger and a yellow paddle was about to be turned over, the yellow ball will travel slowly and you will have to be able to catch or avoid the next ball or missile while waiting to catch the slow yellow ball. So try not to pull the trigger if the paddle is yellow.

In the beginning, the missiles are fired slow enough that you could ignore pulling the trigger and just focus on dodging the missiles that are fired and focusing on moving your hands in the right position to catch the yellow balls.

This is a very unique app and well worth the money if you are interested in improving your reflexes, hand and eye coordination, and focus/concentration ability.

This game is an incredible way to train your visual system and reactions. Nothing else on the general market that comes close to it. Every athlete looking for performance gains should be on this. Also very likely to be be useful people stuck in chronic pain. Not for everyone but 100% my favourite thing in VR
I don’t like the way this game starts out so hard you can’t work levels up and off it started at the level it did then way too hard! I tried to discontinue I couldn’t
As an older athlete, I need all the help I can get to stay focused and “see” the action. REAKT is really helping with that.

developer_response.body: Neuroplasticity is available for athletes of all ages! We are glad this is helping you stay focused and "see" the action. Keep up the great work.

reviews.review_description: The boxing work outs are incredible. They are fast past and push you and mimic exact shots and combinations you would use in a real boxing match. Best part is you switch stances so it even improves your weaker muscles and builds up strength. I'm a few days in and hooked!

developer_response.body: We designed REAKT to be for all sports, but are thrilled to hear that you are finding so many parallels to your boxing practice. Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_description:
I think VR needs more apps like this one - skill building apps, and while it doesn’t offer the same enjoyment as let’s say “beat saber” it’s training you with specific goals in mind.

Love the thought process behind making this app, I love how they keep it fresh with weekly challenges of unique game types, and I try to play it at least a few times a week.

Keep It up devs and I can’t wait to see what the team comes up with next!

developer_response.body: We so appreciate your kind words and you taking the time to leave us a review. Keep up the great work and we look forward to seeing you climb the leaderboard!

reviews.review_description: If you just looking for fungame it not particularly fun, but if you like workout or sports, it’s quite fun and challenging and helpful I guess. I love it for sure.

developer_response.body: The best way to improve a skill is by challenging yourself. That is what we set out to do with REAKT and are glad to hear that you are enjoying the challenge. Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with our community!

reviews.review_description:
Since purchasing i have not been able to play this as it keeps on saying i dont have internet connection. i do and all other games work. 

I have already reached out to support but have not gotten a response. Will refund if I dont hear soon as it is useless if it doesn't work.

developer_response.body: Hey there - So sorry your are experiencing this issue. I understand that you have been in touch with our engineering team and apologize that it took a bit longer for us to get back to you on the holiday weekend here in the United States. Thank you for reaching out!

reviews.review_description:
The graphical style is very suitable, likeable, and nicely done. They've gone for high-end, state of the art gym meets sci-fi. To be more discerning, the environments are better than the animated objects.
Of that there is perhaps some confusable objects as dark grey bombs are too similar to false-triggered targets, which also turn grey.
Voice over is indeed robotic. Either recordings or a warmer sound filter would be welcomed.
Entering multiple sports, age and email is personal data grabbing marketing. Not appreciated.
There seems great feedback after events but there seems no historical calendar of progressions and changes. This seems to be a key oversight. If there is one it is far, far too hidden.
There seems to be a lack addictive, fun gameplay to it - Im not sure I'll keep returning to it as I do with BeatSaber or FitXR. Some more fun-filled variant games and more visible progress might help.
Tracking bombs seems highly problematical. To hit quick targets in near-space is highly prone to obliterate peripheral multi-tracking. For me in those circumstances, I instantly lose all tracking, of all items, every time. It works incredibly against the grain of normal attention. Worse, it is incredibly punishing and demotivating: you hit your near targets, thereby lose all tracking and then it hits you in your FACE with UNAVOIDABLE missiles. This is especially true when near targets are set at knee height, you look down, all your tracking is lost, get unavoidable face slaps...
 Other modes could have jumbo near targets to better track hidden bombs, settings to restrict low shots, no unavoidable bombs - hit & dodge mide, some graphical variations in core interactions, and some more gamification e.g. unlocking things to reinforce your progress.
I want to like this, I do feel it has enormous potential, but currently this feels more like punishment than pleasure or progression.

developer_response.body: Your wide-ranging thoughtful feedback is greatly appreciated. Addressing the historical data comments, overtime you will begin to see graphs showing your competition score progress once you have completed 3 competitions. For individual training (by either Visual Attention or Athletic Awareness) you can currently see where you are by “level” (1-10). Updated voices, more variability, and gamification via "unlocks" are all fantastic ideas we will continue to consider for future updates.

reviews.review_description: The games are well designed, but its hard to track the bombs. I think it would be really nice if there was a mode where you only dodge the bombs, and you can't slow them down.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave us a review. We really appreciate it! Make sure to try our weekly challenges for some of the variety that you are looking for.

reviews.review_title: Bombs not sequenced with catch targets
reviews.review_description: The catch targets are aimed all over the place and the bombs just fly at your face, at a much faster pace. They come in so much faster that it’s near impossible to dodge them.  Half the time a target is down by your knees and the bomb comes out of nowhere at your face.  Or the catch targets are really slow then boom a bomb zooms in.

I’ve had issues with eye hand coordination for over half a century and after a little working with this, I saw my Supernatural performance improve a bit. We’ll see if it helps my martial arts sparring. It would be great to avoid a few more hits to the head than I have been 🙂.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sharing and very cool to hear that you are seeing an improvement with Supernatural. We know they have a great community and it would be cool to see if others feel the same about REAKT!

reviews.review_description: So far, enjoying it immensely. I love the adaptive fast pace, though it's likely traumatic for my family who hear me grunt when I miss and get hit. XD

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback, we love hearing that you are enjoying the pace. Make sure to check back soon for the latest and greatest!
